# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud monitoring and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-instance
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: zones/us-central1-a/machineTypes/n1-standard-1
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      type: PERSISTENT
      boot: true
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-10
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT

- name: cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    region: us-central
    settings:
      tier: db-f1-micro
      backupConfiguration:
        enabled: true
    databaseVersion: MYSQL_5_7

- name: cloud-storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.bucket
  properties:
    location: US
    storageClass: STANDARD

- name: load-balancer
  type: compute.v1.forwardingRule
  properties:
    loadBalancingScheme: EXTERNAL
    target: $(ref.target-pool.selfLink)
    portRange: 80

- name: target-pool
  type: compute.v1.targetPool
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    instances:
    - $(ref.compute-instance.selfLink)
